role_summary: Highlander Folk School music director (1959-2008); folk musician and folklorist whose 1960 introduction of 'We Shall Overcome' to the SNCC founding conference made it the movement's most-cited anthem. Born 1927, died 2015.
ai_reading: Guy Hughes Carawan shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 11 (catalog slug 'candie-carawan-and-guy-hughes-carawan'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Guy Hughes Carawan is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 11 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Guy Hughes Carawan; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Guy Hughes Carawan's Civil Rights History Project oral history is cross-referenced against the Library of Congress published transcript.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 11
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)
